metric_value: SD memory cards are likely to continue becoming a widespread means of storing and transporting digital content.
notes: Kastner as EVP and chief research officer Aberdeen Group

metric_value: This is particularly true given the combination of technology and widespread industry support that the SD Memory Card enjoys.
Over 1,300 SD-enabled products are available across 80+ product categories, leading all other memory card formats.
notes: From Aberdeen 'Consumer Adoption of Memory Cards' qualitative study

metric_value: SD memory cards hold approximately 25% of the US memory card market in 2003.
SD card capacity as shipped in 2003 can hold thousands of images, up to three hours of MPEG4 video, and up to 11 hours of digital music.

SD became the dominant consumer removable flash card format; SmartMedia discontinued by 2006, Memory Stick by 2013, MMC largely displaced, CompactFlash relegated to professional cameras; SD XC reached 2TB by 2024.
